100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 16, 1926, the United States continued to grapple with Prohibition, as debates intensified over the effectiveness of the law amid rising organized crime. In Europe, tensions escalated following the signing of the Locarno Treaties, aimed at securing peace in the post-World War I landscape. Meanwhile, in the world of sports, American swimmer Johnny Weissmuller set a new world record in the 100-meter freestyle, capturing national attention.
